other_observers: none while I was there, although I alerted Gene Rutherford to its location who was nearby. Rumored that Bob Gooycolea also saw and heard the bird.; As far as I know I found the bird first.
specific_location: Hansen Nature Park, the southern section of Fountain Creek Regional Park.
description: flycatcher, larger than Empids, without an eyering. Long wings. Strongly crested. Gray appearing on dorsal view and face. Two EQUAL whitish wingbars. orange bill with lower mandible with only a faint dusky tip. Gray tail. Long primary projection. Underneath light contrasting with dorsal coloration. NOT VESTED. Back with a hint of greenish coloration. Bill wide and stout.Behaviors: Perched in three favorite spots over edge of small pond. Fly-caught frequently often returning to the same choice of three perches. Low, many times below head height and as high as 10 feet above the water. At one time in a chase with another wood-pewee, I think the other was a Western Wood-Pewee, but it never stuck around for comparison.Call: gave both its full song, PEE-a-WEEe, pause... peeooo and on occassion an upslurred paweePlumage: adult
photographer: Photographer: bill maynard, bill maynard, bill maynard
